summary: All 13 checks pass. Live receipt v2 is PASS with receipt_version=2, operator_authored=true, automatic_bot_replay=false, release_promoted=false (trace tg_818730867_20260618T105741Z, message_id=729). Semantic executor transport record is status=completed, role=executor, provider=claude, blocker=null. Two historical failed attempts are fully disclosed in transport.historical_failed_attempts. The prior contradictory replay receipt (tg_934432449, message_id=724, ingress_mode=telegram_bot_api_message_replay_to_live_webhook) is correctly archived as superseded with _superseded marker. Operator Telegram JPEG evidence physically exists at governance/proofs/evidence/BEM934_operator_telegram_tg_818730867_20260618T105741Z.jpg (9602 bytes, valid JFIF). Substantive Claude report at governance/reports/tg_818730867_20260618T105741Z.md contains two idempotency invariants, validation steps, acceptance checks, and disclosed limitations. ACTIVE_QUEUE shows 9/10 done, current_task=BEM934-CLOSE, release_status=FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED. Close preparation receipt is PASS with release_promoted=false. Provider config shows claude_code enabled/priority-1 as primary for all roles; gpt_codex disabled/deprecated with deprecation_reason referencing BEM-934 self-hosted non-existence; gpt_codex_cloud is GitHub-hosted reserve with execution_mode=optional_openai_responses_api_or_mechanical_fallback and requires_self_hosted=false — all three canonical docs (AGENT_CONTEXT.md, SYSTEM_STATUS.md, BEM934_Protocol.md) are consistent. claude.yml workflow_dispatch defines all six inputs (role, provider, trace_id, cycle_id, task_type, task) as required=true with no default values. No file in the evidence set asserts release PASS, and AGENT_CONTEXT.md, SYSTEM_STATUS.md, and ACTIVE_QUEUE all explicitly forbid release promotion until this audit and the final strict validator complete. Top-level PASS in BEM934_live_test_receipt.json is fully consistent with all nested transport records; the previously contradictory receipt is superseded.
